papers.claim: A rigorous methods pattern is to retain an integrated MILP core and add RL plus relax-and-fix modules to improve tractability in end-to-end planning.
papers.evidence: Decision artifact reports full-review support for integrated production/transport/storage/shipping MILP with PPO-assisted acceleration and relax-and-fix (sufficiency 0.78).

papers.claim: Constraint-learning can be embedded into mixed-integer optimization to extend MILP/MIO methods when parts of structure are data-derived.
papers.evidence: Decision rationale explicitly cites learned constraints/objectives with trust-region and ensemble mechanisms, with strong full-review support (sufficiency 0.87).

papers.claim: A practical baseline method for thesis modeling is multi-product, multi-line, multi-period MILP production-inventory planning with explicit operational constraints.
papers.evidence: Artifact states coordinated production-inventory MILP including capacity, shortage, and line-switch constraints, validated via CPLEX case study.

papers.claim: Learning-assisted MILP methods can systematically trade optimality guarantees for speed through prediction-based screening and warm starts.
papers.evidence: Decision card reports exact optimization core plus constraint screening/warm starts/affine-subspace restriction, with quantified speedups (4.3x with guarantees, 10.2x without; sufficiency 0.88).

papers.claim: Matheuristic design combining GA and MILP is a viable methods extension for integrated production-distribution planning.
papers.evidence: Decision evidence indicates direct integrated planning scope and GA+MILP formulation with low uncertainty and sufficient support (sufficiency 0.82).

papers.claim: A MILP-first methods section can be structured by an ML-for-CO taxonomy that screens where learning should intervene in optimization pipelines.
papers.evidence: Decision rationale highlights a methodological framework covering prediction-and-optimization, policy learning, and ML-guided heuristics with moderate certainty (sufficiency 0.78).

papers.claim: Prediction-and-optimization methods can couple ML demand forecasting with dynamic inventory control to outperform classical inventory baselines.
papers.evidence: Artifact reports LR/RF/SVR forecasting linked to dynamic (t,R,S) control and improvements versus EOQ, while noting limited integrated network scope (sufficiency 0.82).

papers.claim: Post-formulation MILP performance can be improved by learning-augmented branch-and-bound components.
papers.evidence: Decision notes method relevance for branching, cuts, node selection, heuristics, and configuration; evidence is method-strong but domain-nonspecific (sufficiency 0.66).

papers.claim: Iterative prediction-and-optimization methods can preserve MILP structure while updating demand estimates through ANN loops.
papers.evidence: Decision artifact reports ANN+MILP iterative framework with endogenous demand-network feedback and scalable two-stage heuristic decomposition (sufficiency 0.86).

papers.claim: Methods for digitally enabled supply chain design can combine formal network optimization with strategic interaction modeling.
papers.evidence: Decision entry indicates objective/constraint network design solved by branch-and-bound (LINGO) and an added evolutionary game layer, with moderate evidence quality (sufficiency 0.78).

papers.claim: Prescriptive ML can be used as an advanced methods layer for repeated mixed-integer convex solve settings to improve feasibility-oriented performance.
papers.evidence: Decision artifact cites OPT-style prescriptive ML in repeated solve contexts with strong relevance to advanced methods and solid support (sufficiency 0.84).

papers.claim: An exact integrated modeling baseline should jointly optimize location, production, inventory, and distribution and benchmark against sequential planning.
papers.evidence: Decision notes direct thesis-core alignment, integrated-vs-sequential comparison, and practical extensions (time windows, multi-echelon, environmental criteria), with high sufficiency (0.86).
